items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 16, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Here are today's biggest stories. 1MDB judgment released The full judgment in Datuk Seri Najib Razak's 1MDB case was released today, describing the scandal as a financial plunder of staggering proportions. Ikram member charged A Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia member has begun facing trial on 158 abuse of power charges involving alleged gratification.

items.title: 'He vanished from sight': Search continues for missing angler who fell off boat
items.summary: Badrul Afiq Jailani, 24, said he panicked when Muhammad Firdaus Zuharsema, 26, who was on the boat with him, fell into the river after their boat collided with floating driftwood about 9.20am. Firdaus fell into the water and I lost control of the boat, causing it to spin in circles. Kuantan police chief Ashari Abu Samah said the incident occurred about 1.5km from the jetty along the river.

items.title: Judge: Najib, Jho Low worked 'hand-in-hand' to siphon 1MDB funds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak was in cahoots with fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho to siphon billions of ringgit in 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds. Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said the then prime minister orchestrated the global-scale financial scheme through wiles and deception of Low, also known as Jho Low, as his proxy. Najib was sentenced to 15 years in jail and fined RM13.4 billion after he was found guilty of all counts of abuse of power and 21 money laundering charges involving RM2.28 billion in 1MDB funds on Dec 26, last year.

items.title: Judge: 1MDB money transferred into Najib's personal account 'not chicken feed'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak's failure to act over the transfer of billions of ringgit linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds into his personal bank accounts was unreasonable. Sequerah said Najib's reaction was inconsistent with that of a reasonable man. He said Najib was not "blind" to the misappropriation of 1MDB funds, but had taken steps to protect his own interests.

items.title: #NSTviral: Lorry driver saves lives by using vehicle to block runaway bus [WATCH]
items.summary: IPOH: A multi-vehicle pile up was narrowly averted along the North-South Expressway when a quick-thinking lorry driver used the rear of his vehicle to absorb the impact of a runaway express bus. Sensing the urgency of the situation, the driver of a nearby lorry ahead positioned his vehicle directly in front of the bus. The lorry driver did an absolutely brilliant job.

items.title: Energy firms brace for 'new era' despite Hormuz deal
items.summary: PARIS: Oil and gas majors have high hopes for a quick reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but they have few illusions about a return to normal for the Gulf energy industry after more than three months of blockage. "A credible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz would be one of the most important developments for the global economy at this juncture," said Claudio Galimberti, chief economist at Rystad Energy. "We're in a whole new era," French expert Philippe Chalmin of the raw materials consulting group Cyclope said this month in presenting its annual market outlook, an industry reference.

items.title: Teacher acquitted of outraging modesty charge
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A teacher was acquitted of an outraging modesty charge after the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against him. Magistrate Aini Adillah Muhammad Faizal today acquitted and discharged Muhammad Irfan Fathullah Mohammad Farizuan, 25, without calling for his defence at the Butterworth magistrate's court here. In delivering her ruling, Aini Adillah held that, upon a maximum evaluation of the prosecution's evidence, they had failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused.

items.title: Najib never showed remorse for 1MDB scandal, says court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak did not show any remorse over his role in the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal, instead shifting blame to other parties. Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said the absence of remorse was a key aggravating factor in sentencing, noting that public anger over the misappropriation must be reflected in punishment given the scale of the offence and its damage to Malaysia's reputation. Sequerah said the 1MDB scandal involved liabilities of nearly RM50 billion, with about RM42 billion already borne by the government, while some RM13 billion remains outstanding despite RM29 billion recovered.

items.title: Anwar's Kazan visit to strengthen Malaysia-Russia, Asean-Russia ties - Envoy
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to meet Rais of the Republic of Tatarstan Rustam Minnikhanov during his two-day working visit to Kazan beginning June 17. Malaysia's Ambassador to Russia Datuk Cheong Loon Lai said discussions during the visit are expected to focus on efforts to expand cooperation in trade and investment, energy, food security, the digital economy, education, culture and tourism. Cheong said Anwar is scheduled to meet Minnikhanov shortly after arriving in Kazan tonight to discuss efforts to strengthen existing cooperation between Malaysia and the republic.

items.title: AI optical parts maker Zhongji Innolight eyes up to US$7bil Hong Kong listing
items.summary: SINGAPORE/HONG KONG: Chinese optical parts maker Zhongji Innolight is planning to launch a share listing in Hong Kong as early as mid-July that could raise up to US$7 billion, two sources with direct knowledge of the matter said. Zhongji Innolight hopes to receive clearance from Chinese regulators for the second listing by late June, one of the people said. TECHNOLOGY LISTINGS BOOST FOR HONG KONG The planned listing would help support the revival in Hong Kong's market for Chinese technology share sales.

items.title: Uzbekistan keen to deepen investment, business links with Malaysia, Asean
items.summary: TASHKENT: Uzbekistan is keen to strengthen investment and business ties with Malaysia and other Asean countries, particularly in high-value-added industries, manufacturing and small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Uzbekistan is open for business from all countries, including Malaysia," he told Bernama on the sidelines of the Tashkent International Investment Forum (TIIF) 2026 here today. Earlier this month, Uzbekistan Ambassador to Malaysia Karomidin Gadoyev said Malaysian investments in Uzbekistan have surpassed US$3 billion (US$1 = RM4.06), underscoring deepening economic ties between the two countries.

items.title: Vantris Energy to shed PN17 status on Thursday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Vantris Energy Bhd, formerly known as Sapura Energy Bhd, is set to be uplifted from its Practice Note 17 (PN17) status on Thursday. The upliftment of the company's PN17 status will take effect at 9am on June 18, according to a Bursa Malaysia filing. The company said Bursa Malaysia's approval of its application for the upliftment marks a major milestone in its recovery journey.

items.title: Fahmi: Malaysia, UK have spent two years in talks over social media regulation
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the engagement involves continuous consultations between Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) and the United Kingdom's communications regulator, Ofcom. Fahmi said Ofcom had, at the early stage of discussions, viewed Malaysia's proposal to introduce a licensing framework for social media platforms through MCMC as a positive initiative. Fahmi said the latest developments in the UK reflected a growing global trend towards tighter regulation of social media usage among children.

items.title: Judge: Najib’s lack of remorse, role in siphoning 1MDB money were factors when deciding his jail, RM13b sentence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — How did the High Court decide on how many years to imprison and how much to fine former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak in the RM2.2 billion 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) trial? Trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who is now a Federal Court judge, today released his 809-page judgment that listed in detail why he had found Najib guilty of all 25 charges in the case. Among other things, the judge said preventing crime would be an more important factor than Najib’s public service, and also said Najib had not shown remorse for his crimes.

items.title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback on BOJ interest rate increase
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Tuesday as investors remained cautious following the Bank of Japan's (BOJ) decision to raise interest rates. At 6pm, the local note fell to 4.0665/0700 versus the greenback from 4.0485/0525 at Monday's close. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the BOJ's decision to raise its policy rate prompted investors to remain cautious despite declining crude oil prices.

items.title: Bar Council Child Rights Committee lauds Anti-Bullying Tribunal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The launch of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal has been lauded as the best mechanism to immediately address the rise in bullying cases among minors in the country. The Anti-Bully Act 2026, aimed at resolving bullying disputes involving minors, officially came into force today alongside the launch of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar to lead M'sian delegation to Asean-Russia commemorative summit in Kazan
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will lead the Malaysian delegation to the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan from June 17 to 18. In a statement today, Wisma Putra said Anwar will be accompanied by Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Minister of Economy Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir as well as officials from the Prime Minister's Office and the Foreign Ministry. The summit is expected to adopt four outcome documents, namely the Kazan Declaration on the 35th Anniversary of Asean-Russia Relations, the Joint Statement on Energy Cooperation, the Joint Statement on Cultural Cooperation, and the Comprehensive Plan of Action to Implement the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership 2026-2030.

items.title: Beshom unit to dispose of Kapar industrial land for RM85.8mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Beshom Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, Hai-O Enterprise Bhd, has entered into a sale and purchase agreement with JT Development Sdn Bhd for the proposed disposal of three plots of freehold industrial land for a cash consideration of RM85.80 million. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the investment holding company said the proposed disposal represents an opportunity for Beshom Group to unlock and realise the value of the land, with a pro forma gain on disposal of about RM52.78 million. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MPB chairman Syed Hussian named Yayasan Tuanku Syed Putra Icon Entrepreneur
items.summary: ARAU: The Raja of Perlis Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Putra Jamalullail has presented the Yayasan Tuanku Syed Putra (YTSP) Entrepreneur Icon Award to Media Prima Bhd group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid. The recognition was given in appreciation of Syed Hussian's contribution to Malaysia's economy and for successfully driving organisational growth and local industry transformation. Also present at the event were Raja Muda Perlis Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail and the Raja Puan Muda Perlis Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.

items.title: Scanwolf secures RM75mil substructure project in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Scanwolf Corporation Bhd (SCB) has secured a RM75 million contract for piling and substructure works related to a 74-storey mixed development project in Jalan Pavilion, Kuala Lumpur. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the company said its wholly owned subsidiary Scanwolf Trading & Construction Sdn Bhd accepted the letter of award (LOA) from Hallson Construction Sdn Bhd. The project is undertaken for Tetuan Armani Hallson Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Timor-Leste's peace journey offers lessons in an uncertain world
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Timor-Leste's transformation from conflict to nation-building offers valuable lessons in peacebuilding, resilience and sustainable development, says Deputy Foreign Minister Datuk Lukanisman Awang Sauni. He said nations that had successfully emerged from conflict and pursued lasting peace could provide important insights at a time when international stability was being tested by mounting geopolitical challenges. Lukanisman said Timor-Leste's experience underscored the importance of reconciliation, strong institutions, inclusive development and international cooperation in securing lasting peace.

items.title: Fahmi: J-Kom chief told to avoid letting govt messages be manipulated
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Newly-appointed Community Communications Department (J-Kom) director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali has been advised to ensure that information conveyed to the public is not open to manipulation by parties seeking to undermine the government. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the reminder came following Hisyamuddin's active and outspoken presence on social media since assuming the post last week. At the same time, Fahmi said J-Kom must exercise caution to ensure the public clearly understands the messages and policies being communicated by the government.

items.title: Pas move to oust Bersatu from PN may backfire, says analyst
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas risks losing support among moderate voters if the Islamist party decides to push for the removal of Bersatu from Perikatan Nasional, a political analyst said. Universiti Malaya political analyst Associate Professor Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said while the Islamist party may gain greater control over the opposition's direction, it risks losing support from moderate voters and those more inclined towards national leadership figures than party identities. "This means Pas cannot unilaterally expel Bersatu from PN.

items.title: MRCB diversifies into AI digital infra with RM2.1bil data centre venture in Bukit Jalil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has today announced its strategic entry into the digital infrastructure sector through a collaboration to develop a state-of-the-art, AI-ready data centre in Bukit Jalil. Bukit Jalil Sentral Property Sdn Bhd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of MRCB Land Sdn Bhd,which in turn is a wholly-owned subsidiary of MRCB, signed a collaboration greement with Perintis Akal Sdn Bhd for the project. Bukit Jalil Sentral, the owner of the 37,320-square-metre leasehold project land, will serve as an asset owner and the master developer of the proposed development.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 16, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its recovery for a second consecutive session, ending higher as investor sentiment strengthened amid improved regional market conditions. Among the most actively traded counters at the closing bell were Tanco Holdings, Pentech and Hong Seng Consolidated. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin climbed to RM271,199, while Ethereum also moved higher to RM7,289.

items.title: RM3mil in illegal e-waste seized in Bukit Mertajam raid, 5 arrested
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Authorities seized an estimated RM3 million worth of electronic waste (e-waste) during a raid on an illegal processing and storage facility here yesterday. The operation, dubbed Op Hazard (E-Waste), was carried out about 11.30am by police from the Seberang Prai Tengah police headquarters in collaboration with the Penang Department of Environment (DoE), Inland Revenue Board (IRB) and Seberang Prai City Council (MBSP). Seberang Prai Tengah police chief Assistant Commissioner Nik Aminuddin Raja Abdullah said inspections revealed that the premises was being used as a storage and processing centre for e-waste materials.

items.title: Anwar to meet Putin in Kazan at Asean-Russia commemorative summit
items.summary: KAZAN (Russia), June 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will lead the Malaysian delegation to the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan from June 17 to 18. In a statement on Tuesday, Wisma Putra said Anwar will be accompanied by Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Minister of Economy Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, as well as officials from the Prime Minister’s Office and the Foreign Ministry. The summit is expected to adopt four outcome documents, namely the Kazan Declaration on the 35th Anniversary of Asean-Russia Relations, the Joint Statement on Energy Cooperation, the Joint Statement on Cultural Cooperation, and the Comprehensive Plan of Action to Implement the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership 2026-2030.

items.title: As US nears birthday, poll shows many Americans doubt it will last another 250 years
items.summary: Some 38 per cent of respondents in the poll - including 40 per cent of Democrats and 26 per cent of Republicans - said they didn't think the US will exist as a single country 250 years from now. Just 62 per cent thought their nation would last. Two-thirds of respondents – including 85 per cent of Democrats and 50 per cent of Republicans – said they agreed with a statement that American democracy was in danger of failing.

items.title: King extends condolences to families of soldiers killed in Gurun blast
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia extended his condolences to the families of two soldiers who were killed in an explosion during training at the Hobart Camp shooting range in Gurun, Kedah today. In a Facebook post, Sultan Ibrahim said he hopes the families of the victims find strength and patience in this difficult time. Sultan Ibrahim, the Malaysian Armed Forces Supreme Commander, also ordered the armed forces to conduct a thorough investigation into the incident.

items.title: Asean must rethink approach to Myanmar conflict, says Timor-Leste president
items.summary: Timor-Leste President Dr Jose Ramos-Horta said efforts to restore democracy in Myanmar would remain futile unless the country's warring parties first agreed to a ceasefire. "Step number one: stop the conflict. "Step number two: stop the conflict, try to normalise the humanitarian situation, bring in NGOs, international agencies, neighbours to help with the humanitarian situation.

items.title: #NSTviral: A cough, a slap and an apology
items.summary: Days after the incident went viral, Zhafri has found it in his heart to forgive the elderly man. The victim shared the update on Facebook today, along with a video showing him shaking hands with the man, days after the incident went viral. The incident was believed to have started after the younger man coughed, which allegedly angered the elderly man.

items.title: Israeli Supreme Court rejects appeal for release of Gaza doctor held without charge
items.summary: Hussam Abu Safiya, the director of the Kamal Adwan hospital, is among at least 14 doctors from Gaza who have been detained in Israel without charge for more than a year. The court based its decision on "confidential materials" that were not shared with Abu Safiya or his lawyer, Naji Abbas, director of the Prisoners and Detainees Department at the Israeli rights organisation Physicians for Human Rights Israel (PHRI), told Reuters on Tuesday. Abu Safiya's lawyer and human rights groups say the medic has been denied sufficient food and assaulted in prison.

items.title: Boy, 4, dies after being found unconscious at Sungai Buloh water theme park
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — A four-year-old boy has died after being found weak and unconscious at a water theme park in Sungai Buloh on Sunday. Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said initial investigations found that the victim is believed to have fallen after leaving the pool area. Mohd Hafiz said so far, investigations have not found any criminal elements, and further investigations are ongoing to determine the cause of death.

items.title: KL, Selangor, Sabah, Sarawak, and more: MetMalaysia’s thunderstorm warning hits most of Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has triggered a thunderstorm alert for several states across Peninsular Malaysia, Sarawak, Sabah, and the Federal Territory of Labuan. Residents are urged to prepare for heavy rainfall, strong winds, and lightning. Affected areas Peninsular Malaysia: Kedah: Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu Perak: Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim Kelantan: Jeli, Kuala Krai, and Gua Musang Terengganu: Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun, and Kemaman Pahang: Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Bera, and Rompin Selangor: Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat Negeri Sembilan: Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin Johor: Segamat, Kluang, and Mersing Federal Territory: Kuala Lumpur East Malaysia Sarawak: Miri and Limbang Sabah: Kuala Penyu (Interior), Ranau (West Coast), Tawau, Kunak, Lahad Datu (Tawau), and Kota Marudu (Kudat) Federal Territory: Labuan Thunderstorm warnings are triggered when rainfall intensity is expected to exceed 20mm per hour, either imminently or persisting for over an hour.

items.title: Scale of 1MDB looting ‘made Attila the Hun look like choirboy’, says High Court judge in 809-page judgement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — The judge who heard Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s 1MDB criminal trial described the scale of the fund’s plunder as so colossal that it made Attila the Hun, a fifth-century ruler remembered for his conquests across Europe, look like a “choirboy”. According to The Edge , Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who sat as the trial High Court judge, made the remark in his 810-page grounds of judgment released today. “The scale of the plunder that took place (financially speaking, of course) made Attila the Hun look like a choirboy in comparison,” Sequerah wrote.

items.title: King extends condolences over death of two soldiers in Kedah grenade blast at Hobart Camp
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has conveyed his condolences to the families of two army personnel killed in an explosion during grenade-throwing training at the Hobart Camp shooting range in Gurun, Kedah, this morning. May they be placed among the faithful and the martyrs,” said Sultan Ibrahim, who is also the Supreme Commander of the Malaysian Armed Forces. Earlier, the Malaysian Army confirmed that the victims were Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar from the 6th Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (6 RAMD), and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip from the 1st Squadron, Royal Army Engineers Regiment (1 RAJD).

items.title: Malaysia looks to Singapore for housing know-how, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: SINGAPORE, June 16 — Malaysia is keen to strengthen cooperation in housing development with Singapore through talent mobility and knowledge-sharing initiatives, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said. He said that through enhanced cooperation, Malaysia stands to gain valuable insights into its own public housing projects from Singapore’s successful models. “Singapore is well known for its public housing system, specifically the HDB model, as well as its Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA).

items.title: Fahmi: Madani communities must step up to ensure only verified information reaches the public
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 16 — Madani Communities nationwide have been urged to ensure the public receives accurate, verified and reliable information on the government’s policies, initiatives and achievements. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the responsibility should be shared collectively and not left solely to government agencies such as the Information Department (JAPEN) and the Community Communications Department (J-KOM). During the event, Fahmi also presented appointment letters to Kelantan Madani Community leaders for the 2026-2027 term.

items.title: Education Ministry targets over 95pc secondary school enrolment as new law comes into force soon
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) aims to raise the universal enrolment rate for secondary schools to over 95 per cent, in line with the Education (Amendment) Act 2025, which will come into force soon. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the success of primary school enrolment, rising from about 92 per cent in 2003 to over 99 per cent in 2026, proved the effectiveness of the compulsory education policy implemented 23 years ago. Last July, the Dewan Rakyat passed the Education (Amendment) Bill 2025, which expands the definition of compulsory education to include secondary schooling.

items.title: Sabah clears January hardcore poverty list, but over 57,000 households remain poor, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 16 — Sabah has successfully removed all 10,825 household heads classified as hardcore poor at the beginning of this year from the state’s poverty registry, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said today. Hajiji said the achievement was recorded based on its eKasih data as of May 31, following a series of targeted assistance programmes carried out jointly by the state and federal governments. He said the reduction was achieved through a data-driven approach involving the Sabah State Database System (PADANS), eKasih records and coordinated intervention programmes.

items.title: MIC to contest four Johor seats in state polls, says party veep Murugiah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — MIC has been allocated four seats in the upcoming Johor state election, said its vice-president Datuk T. Murugiah. He said the matter was confirmed by MIC president Tan Sri SA. “At present, the president has said that we will contest four seats in Johor, while the number of seats MIC will contest in Negeri Sembilan has not yet been finalised.

items.title: Sabah welcomes Japan’s decision to ease travel advisory for parts of east coast as security improves
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 16 — Sabah has welcomed Japan’s decision to downgrade its travel advisory for parts of the state’s east coast, describing the move as international recognition of improving security conditions and growing confidence in the state’s tourism industry. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the revised advisory by Japan’s Foreign Ministry reflected years of efforts by Malaysian security agencies to strengthen enforcement, monitoring and coordination in eastern Sabah. Sabah minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said Japan’s decision to ease its travel advisory for eastern Sabah reflected improved security and stronger enforcement in the area.

items.title: BN unfazed by emergence of new parties ahead of Johor, N. Sembilan polls, says its sec-gen
items.summary: TANJUNG MALIM, June 16 — The formation of new political parties in the country will not affect Barisan Nasional's (BN) prospects in the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections, said BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. “Johor Umno and BN have prepared extensively, and I believe the formation of a new party will have no impact on BN, based on the efforts and preparations that have already been set in motion,” he said. He said this when asked whether the emergence of Parti Wawasan Negara and Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) would pose a challenge to BN in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections.

items.title: Selangor JPJ, MBSJ target cargo bicycles used by foreign nationals in Puchong road safety operation
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 16 — The Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ), in collaboration with the Subang Jaya City Council (MBSJ), conducted an integrated Foreign Driver Operation (Ops PeWA) in Puchong today to detect the use of cargo bicycles by foreign nationals, which are alleged to pose a risk to road users. The operation was carried out following complaints from residents about the use of cargo bicycles by foreign nationals on main roads, including claims that they were using busy routes without regard for the safety of other road users. Among the concerns raised were the use of these poorly maintained bicycles on high-traffic routes and the act of ferrying children in the cargo compartment without safety features.

items.title: Is your child bullying others? New law makes parents legally responsible, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Parents may now be held legally liable for acts of bullying committed by their children under the Anti-Bullying Act 2026, which introduces the concept of shared family responsibility. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the provision is among the key reforms under the Act, extending accountability for bullying misconduct beyond the perpetrator to family members. In contrast, the Anti-Bullying Act incorporates an element of shared responsibility that may bind family members, including in relation to the payment of fines imposed.

items.title: Penang to work closely with Putrajaya on refugee and asylum seeker management, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — The Penang state government is fully supportive of more systematic and controlled management of refugees in the state, particularly the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) mechanism, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. “For the state government, our priority is to ensure public safety, the well-being of local communities and compliance with the law are always preserved,” he said in a statement issued today. “The Penang state government has always adopted a pragmatic, prudent and responsible approach in dealing with the issue of refugees and asylum seekers in the state, in line with the policies and decisions set by the federal government,” he said.

items.title: BN to finalise Johor, N. Sembilan seat allocation next week, says Umno sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Negotiations on seat allocation for Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are expected to be concluded next week, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. The Election Commission (EC) has set July 11 as polling day for the Johor state election, with June 27 as nomination day and July 7 for early voting. For the Negeri Sembilan state election, the EC has set August 1 as polling day, while July 18 is nomination day and July 28 for early voting.

items.title: Johor MB calls on Putrajaya to fast-track JB’s rapid transit project ahead of RTS Link
items.summary: KULAI, June 16 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has urged the federal government to expedite the city centre’s rapid transit project to relieve traffic in the city centre once the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link begins operations on January 1 next year. Onn Hafiz said that the city centre’s rapid transit project project had already been tabled in the Cabinet meeting back in 2024. However, he said the physical work for the project has not yet commenced.

items.title: National Service training programme to be expanded to private universities, says higher education minister
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM, June 16 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is planning to involve private higher education institutions (IPTS) in the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) in the future. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said, however, that the matter is subject to discussions with National Service Training Department (JLKN) director-general Maj Gen Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar. “We will also look at state-owned private higher education institutions, as every state has its own universities.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow: Penang-Perak water deal talks enter final stretch, signing targeted for next week
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — The long-awaited Perak-Penang water transfer project is now in its final stages of negotiations, with a formal agreement expected to be signed as early as next week, Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. He said both state governments have tentatively agreed to conclude and sign the deal by the end of next week in Ipoh, Perak. Meanwhile, Chow said the state government would announce its decision after receiving feedback from the concessionaire of the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project along Karpal Singh Drive.

items.title: Penang orders closure of 177 food premises over hygiene violations
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — A total of 177 food premises in Penang have been ordered to temporarily close for various hygiene violations following inspections of 4,069 premises by the Penang Health Department (JKNPP) from January to April this year. State Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Gooi Zi Sen said the enforcement action involved various categories of premises, including restaurants, eateries, school canteens, hospital cafeterias, and factory canteens, which were found to have failed to comply with established hygiene and food safety standards, based on operations and public complaints. Gooi said JKNPP also received 241 complaints related to food premises this year, with 185 complaints involving the cleanliness of restaurants and eateries, 12 involving supermarkets, nine involving stalls or hawker stands, and eight involving food courts.

items.title: Medicine supply stable in Malaysia despite monitoring of select drugs, says Health Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has assured that medicine supplies in Malaysia remain stable and sufficient, although a small number of products continue to be closely monitored. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry tracks 702 medicine types covering 3,739 registered products through monthly reports submitted by registration holders. “As of May 8, 2026, 505 medicine types, or 72 per cent, had stock levels exceeding 90 days and were classified as low risk.

items.title: Kedah animal feed factory raided over suspected illegal flour storage
items.summary: BALING, June 16 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) Kedah branch raided an animal feed processing factory in the Kuala Ketil Industrial Area yesterday over the suspected storage of wheat flour without proper authorisation. Kedah KPDN director Muhammad Nizam Jamaludin said four enforcement personnel from the Baling branch carried out the operation at about 4.30pm. “The inspection uncovered wheat flour believed to have been used in the company’s animal feed processing activities,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: ‘Same table’ after all? Loke, Onn Hafiz meet in Kulai after public spat over Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook and Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi reportedly met today at the launch of the Shuttle Selatan service in Kulai, after days of public exchanges over political cooperation and federal-state ties. Sinar Harian reported that Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, arrived at the Kulai Railway Station event at 12.45pm, while Onn Hafiz, the Johor Barisan Nasional chairman, arrived about five minutes later. Also present was Johor Public Works, Transport, Infrastructure and Communications Committee chairman Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh.

items.title: Ikram official pleads not guilty to 158 graft charges involving RM98m
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 16 — An official of Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia (Ikram) pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court today to 158 charges of using his position for gratification totalling more than RM98 million between 2021 and 2025. Fakhrudin Abd Karim, 57, entered the plea after all charges were read out during proceedings that lasted nearly two hours before Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin. Under the first 149 charges, Fakhrudin was accused, in his capacity as an authorised signatory to a Maybank Islamic account belonging to Ikram, of using his position to obtain RM81.95 million in bribes for an associate, namely a non-governmental organisation (NGO).

items.title: Federal Court awards RM20,000 each to seven Orang Asli villagers over desecrated ancestral graves
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Federal Court today ruled in favour of seven Orang Asli villagers by ordering that each receive RM20,000 in general damages following the destruction of their ancestral graves. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Nordin Hassan, Datuk Seri Vazeer Alam Mydin Meera and Datuk Lee Swee Seng unanimously allowed the villagers’ appeal and reinstated the earlier decision of the Kuantan High Court. The ruling overturned the Court of Appeal’s October 2024 decision, which had favoured Agrobest (M) Sdn Bhd, a company involved in the prawn farming industry, and two of its employees.

items.title: Marang council says Rohingya learning centre at Telaga Mengkudu legally leased since 2020
items.summary: MARANG, June 16 — The Marang District Council (MDM) has clarified that premises at the Telaga Mengkudu Complex in Bukit Payong, which recently went viral after being alleged to operate as a learning centre for Rohingya children, have been legally leased to a non-governmental organisation (NGO) supported by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) since 2020. In a statement today, MDM said the premises were rented to Persatuan Jaringan Islam Global Masa Depan in accordance with established procedures, strict conditions and regulations for educational and humanitarian welfare activities. The council said it had not received any official complaints throughout the tenancy period regarding breaches of rental conditions that could affect public safety, public order or community harmony.

items.title: Two soldiers killed in grenade explosion during training exercise in Kedah
items.summary: GURUN, June 16 — Two military personnel are believed to have died following a grenade explosion during a training exercise at the Hobart Camp Shooting Range here today. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan, when contacted, confirmed the incident and said police received information regarding the case at about 12.30pm today. Meanwhile, the Ministry of Defence is expected to issue an official statement regarding the incident soon.

items.title: Silat trainee pleads not guilty in KL to sedition over Facebook posts insulting Yang di-Pertuan Agong institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — A silat trainee pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to publishing seditious posts and insulting the institution of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong last year. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nur Shakila Mohd Shariff proposed bail at RM8,000 with one surety, and an additional condition that the accused’s passport be surrendered to the court pending disposal of the case. Judge Tasnim Abu Bakar allowed the accused bail of RM5,000 with one surety, with the additional condition that his passport be surrendered to the court, and fixed July 13 for mention of the case.

items.title: Socso claims could jump 200pc a year with new protection schemes, says HR minister
items.summary: “Through the development of this centre (the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre), we aim to transform the country’s rehabilitation landscape in tandem with technological advancements and the growing need for a more dynamic rehabilitation approach,” he said in his speech at the opening of the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre in Meru Raya here today. The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, opened the centre, which was previously known as the Perkeso Neuro-Robotic Rehabilitation and Cybernics Centre. On the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre, he said the facility, with a treatment capacity of up to 700 patients at any one time, is capable of benefiting 3,000 patients annually.
